notes.sg-intensive-care-medicine-sepsis-diagno: This is a specific type of severe sepsis.
notes.sg-intensive-care-medicine-septic-shock: This is a specific type of severe sepsis.
notes.sg-intensive-care-medicine-surviving-sep: This is a specific type of severe sepsis.
notes.sg-environmental-health-years-of-potenti: This is a component of life expectancy, and the latter is a more general and encompassing term.
notes.sg-environmental-health-life-expectancy: This is a more general and encompassing term than 'years of potential life lost'.
notes.sg-burden-of-disease-global-burden: This is a good label, but the other two are duplicates.
notes.sg-burden-of-disease-national-burden: This is a good label, but the sample titles seem to be about cardiovascular diseases and Alzheimer's, not specifically psychiatry. However, it's not clearly off-domain, so keeping it.
notes.sg-family-medicine-dementia-prevention-i: Merged into the more comprehensive label.
notes.sg-family-medicine-prevention-interventi: Merged into the more comprehensive label.
notes.sg-alzheimers-disease-degenerative-disea: Too general, 'Alzheimer's disease' is already specified in the parent topic.
notes.sg-alzheimers-disease-alzheimers-disease: This is a near-duplicate of 'disease neuroimaging initiative'.
notes.sg-alzheimers-disease-disease-neuroimagi: This is a near-duplicate of 'alzheimers disease neuroimaging initiative'.
notes.sg-cognitive-impairment-memory-impairmen: This is a core symptom/manifestation of cognitive impairment.
notes.sg-cognitive-impairment-montreal-cogniti: This is a specific assessment tool for cognitive impairment.
notes.sg-cognitive-impairment-clinical-dementi: This is a specific assessment tool for cognitive impairment.
notes.sg-magnetic-resonance-imaging-multiple-s: This is a duplicate of 'multiple sclerosis'.
notes.sg-magnetic-resonance-imaging-nuclear-ma: Nuclear magnetic resonance is a broader technique, and functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) is a specific application within MRI that is highly relevant to psychiatry. Given the parent topic is 'magnetic resonance imaging' and the domain is 'psychiatry', fMRI is a more specific and relevant subtopic than the general 'nuclear magnetic resonance'.
notes.sg-diabete-mellitus-peripheral-neuropath: Corrected spelling and merged to diabetic neuropathy
